scenarios.name: Coffee Shop
scenarios.description: Order coffee and cafe drinks easily
scenarios.tip: Cafe Indonesian should stay short and practical for sugar level, takeaway, and familiar iced-drink orders.

scenarios.name: Street Food / Restaurant
scenarios.tip: Street-food Indonesian should prioritize spice, takeaway, bottled water, and simple ingredient or allergy repair.

scenarios.name: Ride App / Taxi
scenarios.tip: Grab and Gojek are part of the real transport flow, so this lane should cover pickup points, stop-here requests, and payment clarification.

scenarios.name: Asking the Price
scenarios.tip: Ask the price early and be ready for cash, card, or QR-payment clarification in markets and small shops.

scenarios.name: Polite Basics
scenarios.tip: Short phrases built around thanks, excuse me, sorry, and soft requests will carry a lot of the first Indonesian pack.

scenarios.name: Convenience Store
scenarios.tip: Convenience stores are high-frequency traveler stops for water, tissues, bags, receipts, and quick payment clarification.

scenarios.name: Hotel / Hostel
scenarios.tip: Hotel Indonesian should cover reservations, check-in, checkout time, air conditioning, towels, and luggage hold requests.

scenarios.name: Directions
scenarios.tip: Directions should bias toward landmarks, hotels, stations, ports, and map-pin confirmation rather than textbook route drills.

scenarios.name: Simple Problems
scenarios.tip: This lane should cover misunderstanding, getting lost, forgotten items, phone trouble, and first-step medical escalation.

scenarios.name: Small Talk
scenarios.tip: Keep Indonesian small talk short and optional so it supports warmth without crowding out the higher-stress service scenarios.

notes: Shared traveler baseline scaffold for Indonesian. No scenario-ID deviation from the family 10-scenario seam; Indonesia-specific changes belong in phrase choice, politeness defaults, payment language, and search alias planning before any runtime wiring.
